record.field: SeaweedFS is a fast, distributed storage system that serves blobs, objects, files, and data-lake tables at the scale of billions of files.

SeaweedFS is a fast, distributed storage system that serves blobs, objects, files, and data-lake tables at the scale of billions of files.

Inspired by Facebook's Haystack and f4 papers, SeaweedFS splits responsibilities between a lightweight master that tracks only volumes and volume servers that store the actual data, keeping metadata overhead to roughly 40 bytes per file and most reads to a single disk seek. A pluggable Filer layer adds a POSIX-like namespace on top of metadata stores such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or Redis, while built-in replication, erasure coding, and transparent cloud tiering cover hot, warm, and cold data. It targets teams that need S3-compatible object storage without the operational weight of larger systems.

Key features
  • S3-compatible API with a built-in Iceberg REST catalog and embedded IAM
  • POSIX-like Filer with FUSE mount, WebDAV, and Hadoop access
  • Rack- and data-center-aware replication plus erasure coding for warm storage
  • Transparent cloud tiering and cross-data-center active-active replication
  • O(1) disk reads with ~40 bytes of metadata overhead per file
  • Encryption, TLS/mTLS, JWT-protected filer, TTL expiration, and Kubernetes support
Best for

Platform and infrastructure teams that need a self-hosted, S3-compatible object and file store for media assets, backups, data lakes, or any workload involving very large numbers of files.

services: master: image: chrislusf/seaweedfs:4.02 command: '-v=0 master -volumeSizeLimitMB=10240 -ip=master -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -port=9333 -mdir=/data/master
' volumes: - ./seaweedfs_master:/data/master restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://localhost:9333 interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 expose: - 9333 deploy: resources: limits: memory: 512M reservations: memory: 256M container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_master' networks: - internal volume: image: chrislusf/seaweedfs:4.02 command: '-v=0 volume -mserver="master:9333" -port=8080 -dir=/data/volume -max=100
' volumes: - ./seaweedfs_volume:/data/volume depends_on: master: condition: service_healthy restart: unless-stopped expose: - 8080 deploy: resources: limits: memory: 1G reservations: memory: 512M container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_volume' networks: - internal filer: image: chrislusf/seaweedfs:4.02 command: '-v=0 filer -defaultReplicaPlacement=000 -master="master:9333" -ip=filer -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -port=8888
' environment: - WEED_MASTER=master:9333 volumes: - ./seaweedfs_filer:/data depends_on: - master - volume restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://localhost:8888 interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 deploy: resources: limits: memory: 1G reservations: memory: 512M expose: - 8888 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8888' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network s3: image: chrislusf/seaweedfs:4.02 command: '-v=0 s3 -filer="filer:8888" -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 -port=8333
' environment: - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID={{ s3_access_key }} - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY={{ s3_secret_key }} restart: unless-stopped deploy: resources: limits: memory: 512M reservations: memory: 256M expose: - 8333 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_s3' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
